---
title: Examples
format:
  html:
    toc: true
---

```{r}
#| include: false
reticulate::py_require(c("matplotlib", "scikit-learn", "plotnine"))
```

## R tinyplot

::: {.plotcat}
```{r}
tinyplot::tinyplot(dist ~ speed, data = cars, main = "Stopping distance by speed", xlab = "Speed", ylab = "Stopping distance")
```

```{r}
#| eval: false
tinyplot::tinyplot(dist ~ speed, data = cars)
```
:::

## R ggplot2

::: {.plotcat}
```{r}
library(ggplot2)
ggplot(iris, aes(Sepal.Length, Petal.Length, colour = Species)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7) +
  labs(title = "Iris sepal and petal length", x = "Sepal length", y = "Petal length", colour = "Species") +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
#| eval: false
library(ggplot2)
ggplot(iris, aes()) + geom_point()
```
:::

## Python matplotlib

::: {.plotcat}
```{python}
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from sklearn.datasets import load_iris
iris = load_iris(as_frame=True)
df = iris.frame
fig, ax = plt.subplots()
ax.scatter(df["sepal length (cm)"], df["petal length (cm)"])
ax.set_title("Iris sepal and petal length")
ax.set_xlabel("Sepal length (cm)")
ax.set_ylabel("Petal length (cm)")
```

```{python}
#| eval: false
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from sklearn.datasets import load_iris
iris = load_iris(as_frame=True)
df = iris.frame
fig, ax = plt.subplots()
ax.scatter(df["sepal length (cm)"], df["petal length (cm)"])
```
:::

## Python plotnine

::: {.plotcat}
```{python}
from plotnine import ggplot, aes, geom_point, labs, theme_minimal
from plotnine.data import mtcars
plot = (
  ggplot(mtcars, aes("wt", "mpg", color="factor(cyl)"))
  + geom_point(size=3)
  + labs(title="Fuel economy by weight", x="Weight", y="Miles per gallon", color="Cylinders")
  + theme_minimal()
)
plot.show()
```

```{python}
#| eval: false
from plotnine import ggplot, aes, geom_point
from plotnine.data import mtcars
ggplot(mtcars, aes("wt", "mpg")) + geom_point()
```
:::
